courseTitle: MentaLIFE: A Framework for Living a Happy, Healthy, High-Quality Life
courseSubtitle: Transform Your Mindset, Manage Stress, and Build Lasting Wellness Habits
description: Workshop-support wellness course based on Kelvin Pruitt's MentaLIFE framework. Designed as a companion to Kelvin's live workshops and conferences, the course reinforces key concepts through short Synthesia avatar videos (client-provided), practical reference tools from the book, and guided reflection. The framework covers three pillars (Mindset Growth, Stress Management, Healthy Habits & Routines) leading to three outcomes (Happiness, Healthiness, High-Quality Living). Structured as 4 zones with 12 video-driven sessions aligned to the Master Outline.
courseRole: Workshop supplement — reinforces concepts from live workshops, provides practical tools, and adds reflection depth. Not a standalone teaching course.

personas.name: The Workshop Attendee
personas.description: A corporate professional (25-55) who attended Kelvin's live workshop or conference presentation and wants to reinforce and apply what they learned.
goals: Review the MentaLIFE framework at their own pace
Access practical tools for stress management and mindset growth
Build a personalized daily intentional living plan
painPoints: Workshop content fades quickly without reinforcement
Needs structured tools to translate concepts into daily habits
Limited time — needs concise, focused content

personas.name: The Wellness Coordinator
personas.description: An HR or wellness professional (30-50) who booked Kelvin for their organization and wants to extend the impact beyond the live event.
goals: Provide employees ongoing access to the MentaLIFE framework
Support organizational wellness initiatives with structured content
painPoints: Live workshops are one-time events with limited follow-through
Needs LMS-compatible content for compliance tracking
Must justify wellness program ROI to leadership

primary: Conference and workshop attendees who saw Kelvin's live presentation
Working professionals in corporate wellness programs
secondary: HR and wellness coordinators seeking supplementary employee development content
Individuals seeking structured personal development frameworks
targetAudience.prerequisites: Recommended: attend Kelvin's live workshop first. The course reinforces rather than introduces concepts.
targetAudience.experienceLevel: Beginner — all concepts are self-contained, but workshop attendance provides richer context

learningObjectives: Explain the three MentaLIFE outcomes (Happiness, Healthiness, High-Quality Living) and the three core pillars (Stress Management, Mindset Growth, Healthy Habits & Routines)
Identify external, internal, and self-inflicted stress patterns and apply immediate deactivation strategies
Differentiate between growth and fixed mindsets and apply the 4-step Mindset Swap technique
Design a personalized Daily Intentional Living plan incorporating mindset habits, stress tools, and routine anchors

instructionalApproach.methodology: Reinforcement learning with reflective practice. Short avatar videos introduce each concept (matching Kelvin's workshop style), followed by practical tools from the book and guided reflection. Document sections provide interactive reference materials (accordions, flashcards, tabs, scenario planners) for deeper application.
engagement: Kelvin's Synthesia avatar videos — personal, direct delivery style
Guided self-reflection exercises with journaling prompts
Mindset Swap 4-step activity (identify → trace origin → reframe → act)
Stress deactivation flashcards for quick reference
Daily Intentional Living Planner (interactive commitment tool)
Foundational tools accordions for browsable reference

assessmentStrategy.formative: Reflection prompts embedded in each lesson — non-graded journaling exercises that reinforce application
assessmentStrategy.summative: Wellness Checks after each zone (70% passing) — scenario-based questions testing application of concepts
assessmentStrategy.practiceOpportunities: Mindset Swap exercise, stress deactivation techniques, Daily Intentional Living Planner

zoneNames: Zone 1: The MentaLIFE Framework Introduction (5 sessions)
Zone 2: MentaLIFE Pillar #1 — Mindset Growth (2 sessions)
Zone 4: Healthy Routines and Habits (3 sessions)

theme.mood: Warm, grounded, empowering — like a trusted mentor conversation

designNotes.designConcept: The Continuum — UI color-grading shifts from cool/gray to warm/saturated as learner progresses
designNotes.branding: MentaLIFE brand identity — wellness-forward, hopeful, evidence-based but accessible
sensitivityNotes: Mental health topic — use trauma-informed, non-judgmental language throughout
Author references Christian faith and Bible verses — present authentically but inclusively
Diverse audience — ensure examples and imagery reflect broad demographics
Avoid toxic positivity — the framework acknowledges struggle as part of growth

fromSource: MENTALIFE MANUSCRIPT PAPERBACK.pdf — Complete 84-page book manuscript
10 ingested content chunks covering all 7 chapters + references
10 video scripts provided by Kelvin (Master Scripts Doc.pdf)
clientProvided: 10 Synthesia avatar videos (to be delivered by client)
toGenerate: 12 lesson content files aligned to Master Outline
Course imagery for document sections (wellness, mindset, nature themes)

notes: Source is a self-published book by wellness consultant Kelvin Pruitt (2024)
CRITICAL: Videos are client-provided Synthesia avatars — NOT platform-generated
Course is a workshop supplement — NOT a standalone teaching course
Workshops are Kelvin's primary revenue; course supports workshops
Kelvin's personal origin story, Blue Zones, Coach Sutherland content removed — covered in live workshops only
Three pillars = methods (Stress, Mindset, Habits); Three outcomes = results (Happiness, Healthiness, HQL)
Zone order follows Master Outline: Framework → Mindset → Stress → Habits
Target duration: ~1 hour (down from 2.4 hours in v1 strategy)
Kelvin has a conference April 21 — soft delivery target
Course designed to be modular — can expand later as revenue grows
Revised from v1 based on client meeting feedback (2026-03-26)
